rulings.Case Title: SHASTA COLLINS VS. HILTON MANAGEMENT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion on Thursday, September 19, 2024 Line 1, PLAINTIFF SHASTA COLLINS AN INDIVIDUAL IN REPRESENTATIVE CAPACITY ON BEHALF OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA AND FELLOW AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES' Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement Plaintiff's unopposed "motion for preliminary approval of class action and PAGA settlement" is granted; hearing required. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MONA MILFORD ET AL VS. D2 PROPERTIES OF FLORIDA LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-23-604855, CGC-23-608547, CGC-23-607017 WITH CASE CGC-22-603644
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion on Thursday, September 19, 2024 Line 8, DEFENDANT D2 PROPERTIES OF FLORIDA LLC, ANDREW NICHOLLS' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-23-604855, CGC-23-608547, CGC-23-607017 WITH CASE CGC-22-603644. Defendants' (D2 PROPERTIES OF FLORIDA LLC, ANDREW NICHOLLS) motion to consolidate is granted in part and denied in part. The court consolidates the following cases for trial purposes: CGC-22-603644, CGC-23-604855, and CGC-23-607017. These cases involve common issues of law and fact regarding the alleged habitability issues at the properties located at 244-246 Divisadero Street and 877-881 Haight Street. The court deems the trial date in CGC-23-607017 (July 28, 2025) as the controlling trial date. Any party is free to seek a different trial date before the presiding judge in department 206. The motion to consolidate is denied as to case CGC-23-608547. This case involves a neighboring property. While there is some overlap with the other actions, a large part of the case relates to how a different landlord (Felink) responding to the fire, which implicates different rights and responsibilities. The parties are ordered to coordinate discovery and motion practice to promote efficiency and avoid duplicative proceedings. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ANH L. TROUNG VS. RUBY TANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Shield Or Redact Defedenats Personal Information From Court Records Pursuant To Crc 2.550
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion on Thursday, September 19, 2024 Line 2, 1-DEFENDANT RUBY TANG's Motion To Shield Or Redact Defedenats Personal Information From Court Records Pursuant To Crc 2.550 Defendant's "motion to shield or redact defendant's personal information from court records pursuant to CRC 2.550" is denied. The motion fails to meet CRC 2.550's stringent requirements and would unduly interfere with the public's right to access court records. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
